msg: PLANNED iter002_warpn256_s2: Switch from WARP_N=128/STAGES=4 to WARP_N=256/STAGES=2 to reduce RHS tile traffic and stage-buffer pressure for skinny-N=512, targeting higher effective bandwidth and occupancy.
PLANNED iter003_regcap128: Constrain register usage with -maxrregcount=128 on the iter001 kernel to raise effective occupancy and hide global-memory latency without changing algorithmic structure.
PLANNED iter004_stage3: Reduce pipeline depth from STAGES=4 to STAGES=3 on the iter001 base to cut shared-memory footprint and synchronization overhead while preserving WARP_N=128 tiling.
PLANNED iter005_tilem256: Increase CTA tile height from MATMUL_TILE_M=128 to 256 on the iter001 base to raise data reuse along M and amortize global-memory traffic for the skinny-N workload.
Baseline SASS extracted via ncu (1114 lines)
Custom SASS extracted for iter001 (1351 lines)
SASS comparison complete for iter001 (saved to tuning/sm90_NVIDIA_H800_PCIe/croqtile/perf/matmul_f16fp32_16384x512x16384/gpt-5-3-codex-high/sass_compare_iter001.json)

PLANNED iter006_warpn64: Reduce MATMUL_WARP_N from 128 to 64 on the iter001 base to lower register footprint and synchronization density per CTA, aiming to improve occupancy-limited memory-bound throughput.
PLANNED iter007_dlcmca: Enable ptxas cache-all policy (-Xptxas -dlcm=ca) to improve L1/L2 reuse for repeated global loads and reduce effective DRAM pressure in the skinny-N matmul.
PLANNED iter008_regcap136: Apply a moderate register cap (-maxrregcount=136) to improve occupancy over the uncapped kernel while avoiding the stronger spill penalty seen with the 128-reg cap.
PLANNED iter009_tilek32_swiz64: Reduce MATMUL_TILE_K from 64 to 32 and match MATMUL_SWIZ to 64 to lower per-stage data footprint and shared-memory pressure while preserving valid swizzle constraints.
PLANNED iter010_fastmath: Add nvcc --use_fast_math to reduce scalar math overhead around the tensor-core pipeline and improve effective throughput under memory-bound execution.
PLANNED iter011_regcap152: Use a light register cap (-maxrregcount=152) to modestly reduce register pressure while minimizing spill risk, targeting better occupancy-latency balance.
PLANNED iter012_warpn96: Change MATMUL_WARP_N from 128 to 96 to test an intermediate N-tile that may reduce per-CTA register/shared pressure while keeping better reuse than WARP_N=64.
PLANNED iter013_warpn160: Increase MATMUL_WARP_N from 128 to 160 to test a wider N tile that may improve RHS reuse and reduce tile-loop overhead for N=512.
Baseline SASS extracted via ncu (1114 lines)
Custom SASS extracted for iter001_tm128_wn128_s4 (1351 lines)
SASS comparison complete for iter001_tm128_wn128_s4 (saved to tuning/sm90_NVIDIA_H800_PCIe/croqtile/perf/matmul_f16fp32_16384x512x16384/gpt-5-3-codex-high/sass_compare_iter001_tm128_wn128_s4.json)

PLANNED iter014_persis_1p1c_wn96_s3: Persistent 1p1c warp-specialized kernel with TILE_M=128 WARP_N=96 STAGES=3 f32 accumulator to match cuBLAS 256x96 tile shape and boost SM occupancy from 14% via SMEM reduction
PLANNED iter015_dma_wn64_s2: Replace TMA async loads with dma.copy.async (L1-cached) to fix 0% L1 hit rate, combined with WARP_N=64 STAGES=2 for 82KB SMEM enabling 2 CTAs per SM

PLANNED iter016_stmatrix_hoist: Enable choreo compiler flags --stmatrix (PTX stmatrix for accumulator store, reduces sync barrier density) and --hoist-offset (hoist loop-invariant address calcs out of K loop) on iter001 base

PLANNED iter017_wn96_s2_2cta: 1p2c TILE_M=128 WARP_N=96 STAGES=2 to reach 106KB SMEM enabling 2 CTAs/SM on H800 (cuBLAS uses 256x96 with ~90KB): occupancy 14.1%->28%, arithmetic intensity preserved via iter001 1p2c structure

PLANNED iter030_choreo_flags_stmatrix_ptxbar: Add Choreo compiler flags --stmatrix --hoist-offset --hoist-scale --ptx-barrier to iter023 config; --ptx-barrier reduces CTA barrier stall (top stall 17246 samples), --stmatrix uses hw stmatrix for output store, --hoist-offset/scale reduces loop overhead

PLANNED iter031_s3_ptxbar_hoist: Reduce STAGES from 4 to 3 with --ptx-barrier --hoist-offset --hoist-scale flags; fewer pipeline stages means fewer prologue BAR.SYNC syncs (3 vs 4), reducing barrier stall in prologue while still hiding TMA latency

PLANNED iter032_tma_cluster_aware: Add --tma-cluster-aware flag to iter030 flags; this is used by all aitune best kernels and may optimize TMA address computation for the cluster topology, reducing long-scoreboard stalls from TMA loads (2nd biggest stall at 10294)

PLANNED iter033_wgmma_arrive_once: Phase 2 CU fix: move warpgroup_arrive() outside inner iv_warp loop - currently called 4x per K-tile causing WGMMA serialization, should be called once before all 4 WGMMAs to enable proper pipelining; directly reduces warpgroup_arrive stall (2566 samples)

PLANNED iter034_wgmma_wait1_overlap: Phase2: change warpgroup_wait<0>() to warpgroup_wait<1>() to allow 1 in-flight WGMMA batch while processing next K-tile; overlaps tensor core computation of tile K with emptying stage for tile K+1; addresses barrier stall by reducing consumer blocking time

PLANNED iter035_wgmma_wait2_overlap: Extend WGMMA overlap: warpgroup_wait<2>() inside K-loop to allow 2 in-flight batches; long_scoreboard is now #1 stall (14536) indicating TMA-to-WGMMA latency gap; more overlap should further hide this

PLANNED iter036_wn256_s2_wait1: WARP_N=256 STAGES=2 with warpgroup_wait<1>() overlap; more WGMMA work per K-tile (8 ops vs 4) to hide long_scoreboard TMA latency; SMEM=224KB fits H800 limit; same wgmma_arrive_once fix applied

PLANNED iter037_s5_wait1: STAGES=5 with wgmma_arrive_once+wait<1>; more pipeline stages reduces long_scoreboard TMA wait (was #1 at 14536); SMEM=224KB fits H800; SWIZ must be updated (SWIZ=128=2*64 still valid for TILE_K=64)

PLANNED iter038_kunroll4_wait1: Phase2: add #pragma unroll 4 to outer K-loop in iter034 CU; forces compiler to expose ILP across 4 K-tile iterations, reducing branch overhead and improving instruction scheduling; dispatch_stall (2966) and math_pipe_throttle (3172) suggest scheduler/issue inefficiency

PLANNED iter039_kunroll8_wait1: Phase2: increase K-loop unroll from 4 to 8; long_scoreboard is #1 stall (21168) indicating TMA latency; larger unroll exposes more ILP and may enable better instruction interleaving around TMA waits
Custom SASS extracted for iter042_cu128_pu32_regs224 (16923 lines)

SASS comparison complete for iter042_cu128_pu32_regs224 (saved to tuning/sm90_NVIDIA_H800_PCIe/croqtile/perf/matmul_f16fp32_16384x512x16384/gpt-5-3-codex-high/sass_compare_iter042_cu128_pu32_regs224.json)

SASS comparison complete for iter042_cu128_pu32_regs224 (saved to tuning/sm90_NVIDIA_H800_PCIe/croqtile/perf/matmul_f16fp32_16384x512x16384/gpt-5-3-codex-high/sass_compare_iter042_cu128_pu32_regs224.json)
PLANNED iter044_stages3_cu128_pu32: Try warpgroup_wait<2> with unroll=128 to allow 2 WGMMA batches in-flight simultaneously, potentially improving IPC from 84% to 88%+ by better hiding barrier stalls
Baseline SASS extracted via ncu (1114 lines)

PLANNED iter046_wn96_stages4: SASS reverse-engineering: cuBLAS uses HGMMA.64x96x16 (WARP_N=96, 48 acc regs vs our 64). Try WARP_N=96 to reduce accumulator register pressure, freeing 16 regs/thread for better ILP and instruction scheduling

PLANNED iter047_spinwait_no_nanosleep: Replace cuda::barrier wait() calls with custom spin loop using try_wait() - eliminates NANOSLEEP instructions. cuBLAS uses SYNCS.PHASECHK.TRANS64.TRYWAIT (non-blocking PTX mbarrier). Implement with: while(!bar.try_wait(token)){}

PLANNED iter048_regs255_nolimit: Test maxrregcount=255 (no reg limit) to let NVCC choose optimal register allocation freely. Our --maxrregcount=224 may force suboptimal scheduling. Also test without -allow-expensive-optimizations

PLANNED iter048_kconst_unroll128: Replace (K+63)/64 with (MATMUL_DEFAULT_K+63)/64 in K-loop bounds to make trip count compile-time constant 256. This enables pragma unroll 128 to generate 512+ HGMMA in static code

PLANNED iter051_tma_evict_first_lhs: Use TMA L2::evict_first hint for A (LHS) loads to immediately evict A tiles from L2 after fetching, leaving more L2 capacity for B tiles. Implement via new PTX inline asm variant tma_load_2d_shared_cta_global_mbarrier_evict_first

PLANNED iter052_split_arrive_wait: Split full[stage].arrive() from full[stage].wait(token) to allow useful work between them: arrive first, then signal empty[prev_stage].arrive() to unblock producer, then wait for full. This overlaps producer unblocking with TMA wait, potentially reducing pipeline stall cycles.
